definition: The U.S. economy operates under fiscal dominance where the 6-7% GDP deficit is the load-bearing variable sustaining the expansion, transmitted through the Kalecki-Levy profit identity (Profits = Investment + Govt Deficit + Net Exports − Household Saving + Dividends). The fiscal contribution to GDP growth is estimated at 1.5-2.4pp.
evidence: Kalecki-Levy identity is an accounting tautology, mathematically certain
Deficit at ~$1.8-2.0T with fiscal contribution estimated at 1.5-2.4pp
Sectoral balance identity (Govt deficit 6-7% = Private surplus 2-3% + CA deficit 3-3.5%) verifiable in Fed Z.1 Financial Accounts
Mundell-Fleming predictions (strong dollar, wide current account, capital inflows) confirmed in data
All four debates affirmed this as the single strongest shared conclusion
open_questions: Is the Kalecki framework functionally unfalsifiable given it explains all anomalies?
Falsification condition: economy grows >2.5% for 4 consecutive quarters while deficit narrows >1pp GDP
Does Ricardian equivalence partially offset the fiscal channel (estimated 30-50%)?
